bias_defense_notes: 5 MCQ positions: pos0=1 (20%), pos1=2 (40%), pos2=1 (20%), pos3=1 (20%). max_share=0.40, constraint max_share<=0.40 satisfied. All 4 positions represented. Short-answer q6 excluded from MCQ position count. Length-bias fix applied 2026-06-14: longest_correct_rate reduced from 0.80 to 0.00 (0 of 5 MCQs have correct answer >120% of mean distractor length). q2 JSON-mode distractor replaced with prompt-level misconception about mentioning format keyword without specifying keys or suppression.

questions.objective_ref: Explain why a language model produces inconsistently formatted output when given no format instruction, and why that inconsistency matters in a Python program.
Write a prompt instruction that requests a specific output format — JSON object, numbered list, or fixed text template — using exact, testable language.
Apply the three format-specification strategies (inline declaration, format example, and template echo) to a prompt of your own.
Identify format drift in a model response by comparing it against the declared format contract.
Describe the output validator pattern — checking format after a response and retrying once before returning a fallback — and explain why it is needed even when format instructions are present.
Write a prompt instruction that requests a specific output format — JSON object, numbered list, or fixed text template — using exact, testable language.
